focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Moretti establishes a command post, gains visibility into the bank, and initiates a direct call via a newly set up line, taking tactical control of the situation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Tactical commander establishing barbershop command post
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Pushes crowd back to secure command post in barbershop, gains visual access to bank interior, and initiates direct phone line to bank
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Establish direct phone line to bank robbers
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: In barbershop command post with visual on bank and direct phone line connected; call answered
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Robbers' response to the initiated police call
evidence_quotes: Moretti has managed to push the crowd back
establishing a clear tactical command post
From this vantage point, he can directly observe the bank and, through its uncurtained door, see partway inside.

status_updates: Establishes tactical command post in barbershop
Gains direct visual access to bank interior

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny revels in newfound control, waving to crowd and media as police lower guns; a newsman lowers a mic from the bank's second floor, amplifying his defiant performance.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Negotiator initiating first phone contact with bank robber
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Begins phone dialogue with Sonny, learns of accomplice Sal, and receives a police radio
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Establish communication and inquire about hostages' safety
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Engaged in phone conversation with Sonny, aware of Sal as an accomplice, and connected to police radio network
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Continue dialogue to assess situation and build rapport
evidence_quotes: MORETTI Okay, you're in there and we're out here. What do we do now?
SONNY I got Sal. MORETTI Sal? What's that for? Salvatore? SONNY Sal. He's the killer.
A cop passing by presses a portable two-way radio into Moretti's hand

relation_updates: Initiates dialogue with Sonny
Integrated into police radio network

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny revels in newfound control, waving to crowd and media as police lower guns; a newsman lowers a mic from the bank's second floor, amplifying his defiant performance.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Negotiator proposing unarmed visit and intervening in police action
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Proposes unarmed visit to build trust, negotiates release of hostage Howard, and intervenes to protect him from police
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Secure release of hostages and build trust for negotiation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Howard released, situation tense due to police intervention, Moretti asserts authority over police conduct
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Sonny's willingness to release more hostages is uncertain
evidence_quotes: I want to come over there, without a gun... and you can frisk me. So you can see you can trust me.
How about letting the people out of the bank. Why put them in this position?
MORETTI Don't fire!
He pulls the cop away from him and starts to give him hell for the rough treatment being given the guard.

relation_updates: Builds tentative trust with Sonny
Asserts authority over police conduct
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Demonstrates willingness to be vulnerable and protective intervention

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny revels in newfound control, waving to crowd and media as police lower guns; a newsman lowers a mic from the bank's second floor, amplifying his defiant performance.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Commanding officer in bank standoff
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Moretti orders police to lower weapons after Sonny's demand, leading to crowd cheers and Sonny engaging with media.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: De-escalate the situation to prevent immediate violence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Police holster guns; crowd applauds Sonny; news media begins covering him.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Moretti's authority is undermined by Sonny's public spectacle.
evidence_quotes: Moretti gestures to the officers to back away, lower the guns.
MORETTI (Cop language command to put gun away) All right - put the guns down! He has to YELL it twice
ON MORETTI unhappy, looking around at Sheldon, who shrugs.

relation_updates: De-escalates tension with Sonny
Seeks support from Sheldon
status_updates: Accepts loss of control to crowd
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Unhappy but decisive under pressure.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Moretti and the Commissioner reaffirm loyalty amid tense orders to prevent a jet's departure, balancing trust, authority, and the consequences of potential error, with underlying emotional and institutional complexity.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Senior police negotiator receiving orders from Commissioner
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Receives direct order to prevent jet takeoff and negotiates for backing in case of honest mistakes
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Has operational order to stop jet and conditional support for errors
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Concern over civilian casualties and protection if mistakes are made
evidence_quotes: COMMISSIONER They jet's comin' out. But don't let 'em off the ground.
MORETTI (pleasantly) Fuck you, sir - if I'm right, I don't need you. What I want is - if I make an honest mistake I want help.

relation_updates: Reaffirms fond, long-standing relationship with Commissioner
status_updates: Entrusted with high-stakes order to stop jet departure
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Cautious negotiator who does not rely on false security

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Leon remains terrified and unable to speak to Sonny, while Moretti waits to connect the call, pressuring Leon to intervene for Sonny's release and his own legal safety.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Interrogator pressuring Leon to contact Sonny
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Pressures Leon to persuade Sonny to surrender after learning the robbery was to fund Leon's sex-change operation
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Get Leon to talk Sonny into giving himself up
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Leon remains terrified and uncooperative, refusing to contact Sonny
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Leon's refusal to talk to Sonny despite Moretti's pressure
evidence_quotes: MORETTI Well, you got to get hold of yourself. You got to talk to him, tell him to give himself up.
So Sonny right away wanted to get me money for a sex change operation: but where was he to get that? 2500 dollars!
So when I hear he's in the bank, I almost go crazy because I know he's doin' it for me.

relation_updates: Pressures Leon by framing him as legally implicated
Establishes adversarial dynamic with Leon
status_updates: Gains crucial intelligence about robbers' motives and relationship

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The bank loses power, leaving only emergency lights; Sylvia uses hand lamps, SAL and Sonny react, phones fail, and Moretti's urgent bullhorn call from outside shifts focus to Sonny as he moves toward the door.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: On-scene commander adapting tactics
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Switches to bullhorn communication when phone lines fail during power outage
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To prompt Sonny to exit the bank for direct engagement
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Calling for Sonny via bullhorn from outside the bank
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Phone communication disrupted; outcome of bullhorn call pending
evidence_quotes: Sonny hurries to a nearby phone to contact Moretti, but the phones are dead.
They then hear Moretti's voice through a bull-horn from outside: 'Sonny... Sonny... Come out a minute... Come out a minute...'

relation_updates: Uses bullhorn for direct, public appeal to Sonny
status_updates: Adapts communication method due to infrastructure failure

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Moretti and Sheldon discuss control over a situation at Kennedy, with Sheldon asserting federal jurisdiction since 3:07, while Moretti expresses fear of making a mistake.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Negotiator expressing fear of error
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Moretti admits fear of making a mistake, leading Sheldon to offer federal takeover
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sheldon has proposed assuming federal control, challenging Moretti's operational authority
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Sheldon will take over and how Moretti will respond
evidence_quotes: What makes you think you'll be able to control it?
He hasn't so far. I'm the one who can make a mistake. That's what scares the shit out of me.

relation_updates: Sheldon asserts federal jurisdiction and proposes assuming control
status_updates: Reveals personal anxiety about potential error in operation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny, injured and shaken, demands a helicopter and jet to escape; he negotiates with Moretti, offering hostages in exchange for his wife and transport, suspecting betrayal.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Police negotiator during bank robbery standoff
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Remains calm after Sonny is attacked by a bystander, then negotiates a hostage exchange for a helicopter, jet, and Sonny's wife
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Secure hostage releases by bargaining with Sonny's escape demands
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Terms established for staged hostage release in exchange for transport and Sonny's wife, with mutual distrust
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether police will meet demands for helicopter and jet, and ensure safe hostage releases
evidence_quotes: MORETTI Sonny, ya want somethin'?
MORETTI Okay, what do you give me?

relation_updates: Sonny explicitly distrusts Moretti
Establishes hostage exchange framework
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Projects calm authority, uses casual demeanor to manage crisis

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny confronts Moretti, skeptical of his promises; Moretti assures him the bus, jet, and wife are coming, and agrees to deliver pizza, drinks, and aspirin for those inside.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Police negotiator addressing hostage-taker's demands
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Assures Sonny that bus, jet, and wife are coming, and agrees to send pizza, drinks, and aspirin to hostages
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Secure Sonny's cooperation by promising requested items and providing immediate concessions
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sonny accepts the promises and re-enters the bank
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Fulfillment of promises regarding bus, jet, and wife
evidence_quotes: MORETTI Wait a minute... everything you asked for is on the way...
MORETTI Sonny, your wife's on the way... We reached her... your wife's on the way... everything you asked for, you got.
MORETTI Aspirins... okay you got it. (turns behind him to a near-by cop) Charlie! Six pizzas!
SONNY Okay... Sonny turns and walks back into the bank.

relation_updates: Establishes negotiating rapport with Sonny
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Direct negotiator using assurances and quick action to de-escalate

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Eugene Moretti serves as the primary police negotiator during a Brooklyn bank robbery, establishing direct contact with robber Sonny Wortzik and managing the evolving crisis. He navigates complex dynamics with FBI counterparts, learns the robbers' personal motives, and adapts tactics as the situation escalates to an airport standoff. Throughout, Moretti balances operational control with relationship-building, ultimately receiving high-stakes orders to prevent the robbers' escape.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sal, Sonny, and Jackie wait near a bank; Sonny checks the time, Sal exits the car and walks toward the bank as Sonny slowly follows, preparing for a planned action.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Watchful accomplice preparing for the robbery.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sal exits the car and walks toward the bank after Sonny's cue.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sal is walking toward the bank.
evidence_quotes: he checks his tie's alignment, shoots his cuffs and is ready...
Sonny checks his watch, turns to Sal in back seat: SONNY seconds, Sal...
Sal exits car, walks toward bank.

status_updates: moved from car to bank vicinity
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Watchful and intense individual dressed in a suit.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie abandons the robbery, leaving Sonny to take his gun; Sonny, deeming Howard useless, takes keys, frees Jackie, then relocks the door after a sudden afterthought.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Armed robber confronting the bank manager with a machine pistol.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sal produces a machine pistol and points it at Mulvaney, instructing him to act normally.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sal has threatened Mulvaney with a gun and taken control of the situation.
evidence_quotes: Sal now stands up, moves to the side of Mulvaney's desk.
producing as he does a machine pistol, which he holds on Mulvaney's chest
SAL Just go on talking, like nothing was happening, okay?

relation_updates: established adversarial relation with Mulvaney
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Threatens with a machine pistol and gives orders to maintain facade.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny decides to take a married woman with kids as a hostage to deter police violence, warns Mulvaney not to cross him, and returns to Sal; all phones are off the hooks.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Armed lookout providing cover for Sonny.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny puts down his rifle and looks to Sal for coverage; Sal alerts Sonny to a man approaching the bank from across the street.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sal remains positioned as the armed lookout, now aware of a potential external threat.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: A man in a business suit is walking directly toward the bank, identified by Sal as suspicious.
evidence_quotes: Seeing that, Sonny puts it down and looks over to Sal for coverage. SONNY Sal...
SAL (looking past camera, falling onto the floor behind Mulvaney) Sonny... who's that? Across the street.

relation_updates: Sonny explicitly trusts Sal to provide armed cover
status_updates: Becomes primary armed guard while Sonny is unarmed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Armed partner Sonny trusts to provide protection during negotiations.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny decides to take a married woman with kids as a hostage to deter police violence, warns Mulvaney not to cross him, and returns to Sal; all phones are off the hooks.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Bank robber aiming a gun at an approaching outsider.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sal raises his gun to shoot an approaching man, showing anticipation and a slight smile.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sal remains armed after the man leaves, with the immediate external threat gone.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Sal's readiness to use lethal force persists.
evidence_quotes: Sal brings the gun up so he can shoot the man, at the same time, crabbing himself aside so he is concealed behind Mulvaney and the desk.
SAL He's gone?

relation_updates: Takes directive role over Mulvaney to eliminate external threat
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Exhibits a relaxed, anticipatory posture when preparing to shoot.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny revels in newfound control, waving to crowd and media as police lower guns; a newsman lowers a mic from the bank's second floor, amplifying his defiant performance.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Armed enforcer guarding hostages.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny tells negotiator Moretti that Sal is 'the killer' and that they are Vietnam veterans during a phone call.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Police negotiator Moretti receives information about Sal's role as 'the killer' and his Vietnam veteran status, with details shared via police radios.
evidence_quotes: SONNY Sal. He's the killer. We're Vietnam veterans so killing don't mean anything to us, you understand?

relation_updates: Sonny establishes Sal as his lethal confederate to authorities
status_updates: Positioned in vault area monitoring hostages
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Identified by Sonny as 'the killer' and a Vietnam veteran.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny plans escape abroad, asks Sal where to go; Sal jokingly says Wyoming. Sonny leaves but returns to hand Sal his gun before heading to the front door.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Partner who rejects negotiation and upholds suicide pact.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sal interrupts Sonny to reject any plea deal, states he will never return to prison, and reminds Sonny of their suicide pact, asking if it still holds.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sal has firmly rejected negotiation and reaffirmed the suicide pact with Sonny.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Sonny confirms the pact is still active.
evidence_quotes: What kind of a deal?
...because if you're talking about coppin' a plea, I'm tellin' you right now, there's no deal... I'm never going back to prison...
Do you remember the pact we made? You and me and Jackie - that night in the bar...
Does that still go?

relation_updates: Sal reinforces his bond with Sonny through the suicide pact
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Committed to dying rather than returning to prison.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 32、INT. BANK - LATE AFTERNOON - APPROX. 5 PM - MAIN BANK AREA
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: During a bank robbery, Sylvia lights a cigarette against Sal's disapproval, blaming him if she dies of cancer; Sonny exits upon learning police are bringing in his wife.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Robber who values bodily purity and health.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sal argues against Sylvia smoking, citing purity and health concerns, and receives the rifle from Sonny before Sonny exits.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sal holds the rifle and is positioned by the bank door after Sonny leaves.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Sylvia decides to smoke despite Sal's objections.
evidence_quotes: SAL You don't smoke... why do you want to start now.
SAL Because I don't want cancer.
SAL Your body is the temple of the Lord.
SAL (exiting) No - it's because you're weak.

relation_updates: Shows care for Sylvia's well-being by opposing her smoking
Receives rifle from Sonny, indicating increased trust and responsibility
status_updates: Now in possession of Sonny's rifle
Positioned at the door after Sonny leaves
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Believes in maintaining bodily purity and opposes smoking for health reasons.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny and Sal react to a TV report misrepresenting them as homosexuals during a hostage situation; Sonny dismisses the media focus as uncontrollable and irrelevant while questioning the delayed arrival of the jet.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Bank robber sensitive to media portrayal of his sexuality.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sal protests the media labeling him as homosexual and asks Sonny to intervene, but Sonny dismisses the issue as uncontrollable.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sal's request is denied by Sonny, and he is told to drop the matter.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The media continues to portray Sal as homosexual.
evidence_quotes: SAL They keep sayin' two homosexuals. I'm not a homosexual. I want you to stop them saying that.

relation_updates: Seeks Sonny's help to stop the media portrayal, but Sonny dismisses it as uncontrollable
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sal is concerned about public perception of his sexuality.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A figure revealed in the street is Sonny's mother, prompting his reluctant exit from the bank despite his frustration, as Sheldon calls out to him via bullhorn.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Hostage with a traumatic past, skeptical of Sonny.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny discloses Sal's prison gang-rape to the group, and Sal responds with implacable tension and distrust towards Sonny.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sal is seated in the conference room, remaining unsmiling and serious amidst the group's dynamics.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Sal's doubt about Sonny's loyalty and the escape plan.
evidence_quotes: CLOSEUP - SAL looking at him. Implacable.
Sal and Edna alone remain unsmiling.

relation_updates: Tension with Sonny over loyalty
Past trauma exposed to hostages
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Carries trauma of prison gang-rape at age 13.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sylvia and Mulvaney reunite joyfully; Sonny is arrested, emotionally breaking down; Leon doubts Sonny's prison survival, calling him loud and hard to live with, while Heidi recalls his humor.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Armed accomplice in limo during airport approach.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Smiles believing escape is possible, then reacts too slowly to driver's gun.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Driver has gun leveled at Sal.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Immediate threat from driver's gun.
evidence_quotes: For the first time he smiles. He realizes that escape is within their grasp.
Sal, open-mouthed with amazement, is a split-second late bringing his gun up.
Driver completes his turn and has his gun leveled at Sal between Miriam and Margaret in middle row.

relation_updates: Maria gives Sal her rosary out of concern
Murphy reminds Sal to keep gun ready
status_updates: smiles for first time believing escape is possible
reacts too slowly to driver's gun
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Hopeful smile before being ambushed.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sylvia and Mulvaney reunite joyfully; Sonny is arrested, emotionally breaking down; Leon doubts Sonny's prison survival, calling him loud and hard to live with, while Heidi recalls his humor.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Armed accomplice in limo during FBI ambush.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Shot multiple times by the driver and dies in the limo seat.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Dead from gunshot wounds in the limo.
evidence_quotes: Sal, hit, slams back into the seat; the gun flies upward.
Another SHOT hits him, flinging his head back.
He stares sadly, into Sonny's eyes. As though he knew it all along -- betrayed. And dies.
Sal's body lies slumped.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Shot while seated in the limo during the FBI ambush.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny bids farewell to hostages at the airport; they move on, forgetting him. Rain falls as he smiles. Leon is now Lana, Heidi lives on welfare with children. Sonny serves 25 years in prison.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Deceased bank robber.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sal's dead body is carried away from the scene, and his mother Pauline is interviewed, stating she learned of his homosexuality only after his death.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Deceased.
evidence_quotes: THROUGH THE SHOT Sal's body is carried, Sonny, seeing it out of the corner of his eye.
PAULINE No, not until after they killed him.
PAULINE Oh, a long time. Because I kept asking my husband where the heck could Junior be?

relation_updates: Sal's mother, Pauline, reveals she didn't know he was homosexual until after his death.
The hostages forget about him as they move on.
status_updates: His body is removed from the scene.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Remembered by his mother as 'Junior' with whom she had little recent contact.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Sal, a Vietnam veteran and bank robber, participates in a heist with Sonny. He evolves from a watchful accomplice to a committed partner who takes on increasing responsibility, revealing personal vulnerabilities and a traumatic past. Ultimately, he is killed during the escape attempt, leaving behind unresolved questions about his identity and motivations.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jackie abandons the robbery, leaving Sonny to take his gun; Sonny, deeming Howard useless, takes keys, frees Jackie, then relocks the door after a sudden afterthought.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Bank robbery leader handling a team member's desertion
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jackie abandons the heist, giving his gun to Sonny, who then secures Howard and locks the bank.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Continue the robbery with Sal while controlling hostages
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sonny and Sal are the only robbers left in the locked bank, with Sonny now armed with two guns
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to manage the heist with reduced manpower
evidence_quotes: I'm sorry, Sonny... I can't make it...
Jackie, with a last apologetic glance, gives his gun to Sonny and vanishes into the sweltering afternoon.
Sonny sees that Howard is useless, so he runs to Howard, grabbing the keys from him and pulls Howard along with him to the front door.

relation_updates: Jackie exits the operation
Sonny takes direct control of Howard
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Leader who takes direct control when plans go wrong

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny decides to take a married woman with kids as a hostage to deter police violence, warns Mulvaney not to cross him, and returns to Sal; all phones are off the hooks.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Robber preventing alarm and discovering low cash
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny stops Mulvaney from using a spur key that would trigger an alarm, then finds the vault contains only about $4,000 in small bills.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Secure the available cash and handle immediate bank communications.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sonny has the minimal cash in a bag and is responding to a ringing phone, with the heist severely limited by insufficient funds.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to proceed with the heist given the drastically reduced amount of money.
evidence_quotes: Sonny quickly reaches out and grabs Mulvaney's hand, and looks at the key he has extended.
SONNY What the fuck you tryin' to do? Trip the alarm? Use the spur key?
SONNY This is it? What am I gonna do with this? Holy shit!

relation_updates: Sonny threatens Mulvaney to ensure compliance
Sonny shows unexpected compassion to Miriam
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Aggressive but adaptable robber who adjusts to setbacks and shows unexpected compassion.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny decides to take a married woman with kids as a hostage to deter police violence, warns Mulvaney not to cross him, and returns to Sal; all phones are off the hooks.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Robber during first police contact and hostage complication
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny discovers an extra hostage, Maria, in the ladies' room and receives a phone call from Detective Sergeant Moretti, establishing direct police communication.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Manage hostages and respond to police demands
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: In phone conversation with Moretti, aware of police surveillance, and with an additional hostage to control
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to handle police negotiation and the extra hostage, Maria
evidence_quotes: SONNY Who the hell is that? God damn it! Whatthe...
MULVANEY (to Sonny) It's for you.
SONNY (into phone) Yeah.
MORETTI (V.O.) What are you doin' in there?

relation_updates: Adversarial relationship established with police (Moretti)
status_updates: Police are now aware and in contact
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sonny is cautious and frustrated, directly engaging with police threats

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny revels in newfound control, waving to crowd and media as police lower guns; a newsman lowers a mic from the bank's second floor, amplifying his defiant performance.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Robber who publicly confronts police, forcing them to lower weapons, and gains crowd and media attention.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny forces police to lower their weapons by leveraging crowd and media presence, receives crowd applause, and begins interacting with media cameras.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To use public and media attention to pressure police into compliance.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Police have lowered their weapons, crowd is supportive, and media is actively covering Sonny, giving him a public platform.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Standoff continues with police having complied under public pressure.
evidence_quotes: He takes a defiant step into the street.
SONNY (screaming) Attica! Attica! Go ahead! Blow off the front of the whole God damn bank!
MORETTI (Cop language command to put gun away) All right - put the guns down!
He turns and waves to the media.

relation_updates: Police forced to retreat and lower weapons
Crowd becomes supportive audience
Media begins actively covering him
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Defiant individual who uses public confrontation and media to gain control.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny plans escape abroad, asks Sal where to go; Sal jokingly says Wyoming. Sonny leaves but returns to hand Sal his gun before heading to the front door.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Robber who uses live TV interview to justify actions and communicate during standoff
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny gives a live TV interview, explaining robbery motives due to union job barriers and low pay, challenges the newsman's questions, and notes media attention as beneficial for their situation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sonny is engaged in broadcasting his message on live TV while in the bank.
evidence_quotes: SONNY (to Sal) The whole media is out there... it looks a lot better for us than it did before...
SONNY I don't know... It's where they got the money. I mean, if you want to steal, you go to where they got the money, right?
SONNY Wait a minute... I'm talkin' to you. I'm askin' you a question...
SONNY Yeah! We're hot entertainment, right? You got me and Sal on TV...

relation_updates: Engages directly with media as platform
Trusts Sal with gun for planning
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Spokesperson who articulates economic grievances publicly during crisis

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Leon remains terrified and unable to speak to Sonny, while Moretti waits to connect the call, pressuring Leon to intervene for Sonny's release and his own legal safety.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Motivated by the need to fund Leon's sex change operation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Leon discloses that Sonny's bank robbery is to pay for his sex change operation, costing $2500.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Obtain $2500 for Leon's sex change operation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sonny's motive for the robbery is understood as personal and medical, not purely financial.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Sonny can secure the money and help Leon undergo the operation.
evidence_quotes: So when I hear he's in the bank, I almost go crazy because I know he's doin' it for me.
So Sonny right away wanted to get me money for a sex change operation: but where was he to get that? 2500 dollars!

relation_updates: Establishes marital relationship with Leon
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A partner committing bank robbery to finance a loved one's gender-affirming surgery.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny is urged to surrender as his mother recalls his infancy with tearful nostalgia, expressing lost hopes while he distances himself toward the bank door.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Sonny discusses his harmful impact on loved ones with his mother and hears of his father's disownment.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny states he is a 'fuckup and outcast' who hurts those he loves, learns his father says he is dead, embraces his mother, and returns to the bank.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sonny walks back to the bank door after embracing his mother and acknowledging his father's rejection.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Father's disownment and its effect on Sonny.
evidence_quotes: I'm a fuckup and an outcast.
He says you're dead. He says he doesn't have a son.
He's right. You shoulda done what he did. Go home.

relation_updates: Father has disowned Sonny
Sonny embraces mother despite tensions
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sonny sees himself as a destructive outcast who harms loved ones.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny dictates his will, leaving money to Leon and Heidi, entrusting Jimmy, forgiving his mother, requesting a military funeral, and asking Sylvia to type and keep it confidential.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Sonny dictates his will to settle personal affairs.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny dictates a will leaving money to Leon for a sex change, to Heidi and their children, forgiving his mother, and requesting a military funeral.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To settle affairs before potential death.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sylvia types the will for Sonny's signature.
evidence_quotes: Sonny then declares he wants to dictate his will and asks if they need a notary.
To his 'sweet wife, Heidi,' he leaves $5,000, declaring her the only woman he ever loved, and reaffirming his love in this 'sad moment.'
To his mother, he asks forgiveness, acknowledging she never understood him, but stating he is who he is.

relation_updates: Formal bequests to Leon and Heidi
Asks forgiveness from mother
status_updates: Sonny shifts from negotiator to someone preparing for death
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sonny, a man preparing for death by settling personal debts.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny searches the Driver, finds drugs but ignores them, asserts control by choosing Murphy as driver, positions the limo for the heist, and enters the bank with Murphy while FBI watches.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Sonny selects the driver after gaining leverage over the original driver and defying the FBI.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny frisks and rejects FBI agent Murphy, selects the original driver, discovers and hides the driver's drugs to create a covert bond, then chooses Murphy as the actual driver.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To secure a driver he can control for the heist.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sonny has Murphy as the driver, and the FBI has complied with his choice after he asserted control.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Murphy will cooperate and if the FBI will accept Sonny's terms.
evidence_quotes: SONNY I want him. He points at Murphy.

relation_updates: Sonny establishes a covert understanding with the driver by hiding his drugs
Sonny asserts dominance over Sheldon by rejecting Murphy and demanding his own choice
status_updates: Sonny gains a compliant driver who is now indebted to him
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sonny uses discovered secrets to manipulate and assert dominance.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sylvia and Mulvaney reunite joyfully; Sonny is arrested, emotionally breaking down; Leon doubts Sonny's prison survival, calling him loud and hard to live with, while Heidi recalls his humor.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Bank robber in a standoff with FBI agents
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny is disarmed by Sheldon when the FBI moves to intervene.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Disarmed and vulnerable to arrest.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The immediate outcome after losing control of his weapon.
evidence_quotes: CLOSE - SONNY looking at the plane, grasping his money and his gun. The rifle stands straight up between his legs.
Sheldon brings up his hands to reach through the open window to grab Sonny's rifle barrel, pushes it away, aiming out windshield.
Sonny lets it go.

relation_updates: Hostages reunite joyfully, moving past the incident
Leon voices negative judgment on Sonny's character
Heidi recalls Sonny's humor fondly
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Alert and reactive but overcome in the confrontation.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sonny bids farewell to hostages at the airport; they move on, forgetting him. Rain falls as he smiles. Leon is now Lana, Heidi lives on welfare with children. Sonny serves 25 years in prison.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Manacled prisoner at airport saying goodbye to former hostages
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sonny shouts goodbye to hostages as they depart; they respond with forced smiles, already forgetting him. Rain falls, and he smiles.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Manacled and in FBI custody, being taken away
evidence_quotes: BACK TO SONNY manacled and helpless. He is twisted into some yet more uncomfortable position by the uncaring Agents.
SONNY Goodbye! You were terrific! Mouth! You're beautiful! See you!
But the everlasting smile overtakes him...

relation_updates: Hostages react with forced smiles and awkwardness
Leon expresses relief at Sonny's imprisonment
Heidi reflects on chaotic life with Sonny
status_updates: Manacled and handled roughly by agents
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Man in custody accepting his situation with a smile

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Sonny Wortzik leads a bank robbery that quickly unravels when an accomplice flees and the vault yields little money. He adapts by taking hostages, engaging in lengthy negotiations with police and the FBI, and using media attention to broadcast his motives. The standoff ends with his arrest after a failed escape attempt, resulting in a 25-year prison sentence.
